does: This paper describes the implementation of a plugin architecture for ChatGPT that allows the model to extend its capabilities beyond text generation by connecting to external tools and services. The system enables ChatGPT to access real-time information, perform computations, and integrate with third-party APIs while maintaining safety as a foundational design principle. The implementation provides a structured interface through which the language model can invoke external functionality while constraining how and when such invocations occur, addressing key limitations of purely text-based models regarding currency of information and computational accuracy.
connects: This work builds on prior research in tool-use for language models, extending approaches where models learn to call functions or APIs to accomplish tasks. It connects to safety research in AI systems that emphasizes controlled integration of language models with external systems, building on discussions about alignment and oversight in agentic AI systems. The plugin architecture responds to identified limitations in large language models regarding real-time knowledge and factual grounding, problems that have motivated research into retrieval-augmented generation and information-seeking behaviors in language models. The safety-first design philosophy reflects emerging best practices in deploying capable AI systems with external access.
enables: This architecture enables a new class of applications where language models can serve as intelligent intermediaries between users and external systems, opening possibilities for AI assistants that maintain context while reliably accessing current data, executing computations, or controlling real-world services. It establishes a template for safe tool use in language models that could inform broader approaches to AI system integration and compositional AI. The framework potentially enables research into how models learn to delegate tasks appropriately, reason about tool capabilities and limitations, and handle the verification and validation of external system outputs, advancing understanding of trustworthy agentic AI systems.
